title: Inferred Neutralization-State Expert
summary: F1 strategy sources stress that Safety Car/VSC periods sharply change pit economics and teams pre-arm pit windows for them.
rationale: F1 strategy sources stress that Safety Car/VSC periods sharply change pit economics and teams pre-arm pit windows for them. Because the dataset has no explicit SC/VSC flag, a latent neutralization state inferred from field-wide lap-time shocks and synchronized pit activity could explain many otherwise noisy next-lap stops.
implementation_hint: Within each Race x Year x LapNumber snapshot, build robust aggregates such as median/dispersion of LapTime (s), median/dispersion of LapTime_Delta, share of cars with extreme slow laps, and share pitting now. Turn these into a neutralization score/posterior and either interact it with tyre/race-progress features or train a separate green-flag vs neutralized expert and blend them.
expected_effect: Should improve recall on abrupt pit clusters and reduce public-LB misses from caution-heavy races that static tyre-window features under-model.
risk: If same-lap field aggregates are not available at decision time, they become subtly leaky; if uncertain, lag them by one lap. Also validate by whole-race blocks because neutralization patterns are highly race-specific.
